title: Load the current todo list on app start
description: This test verifies that opening the app shows a loading state and then displays the current todo list.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Verify a loading state is visible
Verify the todo list is displayed

title: Add a new todo from the home page
description: This test verifies that a user can create a todo from the main page and see the new item appear in the list.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill in the todo input with a new task description
Submit the new todo
Verify the new todo appears in the list

title: Delete an existing todo from the list
description: This test verifies that a user can remove an existing todo and see it disappear from the list.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Verify the todo list is displayed
Select a todo for removal
Verify the selected todo disappears from the list

title: Mark a todo as complete
description: This test verifies that a user can mark an existing todo complete and see the completed state update in the list.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Select an incomplete todo's completion control
Verify the todo is shown as completed

title: Add a new todo by pressing Enter
description: This test verifies that a user can create a todo using the keyboard and see the new item appear in the list.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill in the todo input with a new task description
Press Enter
Verify the new todo appears in the list

title: Mark a todo as incomplete again
description: This test verifies that a user can toggle a completed todo back to incomplete and see the state update in the list.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Select a completed todo's completion control
Verify the todo is shown as incomplete

title: Handle loading state while todos are being fetched
description: This test verifies that the app clears its loading state and renders the list after the initial todo fetch completes.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Verify a loading state is visible
Verify the loading state disappears and the todo list is displayed

title: Reject an empty todo submission
description: This test verifies that submitting an empty todo does not add a new item and shows validation feedback.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Leave the todo input empty
Submit the new todo
Verify a validation error is visible
Verify no new todo is added

title: See a delete error message when removal fails
description: This test verifies that when a todo deletion cannot be completed, an error message appears at the top of the page.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Verify the todo list is displayed
Select a todo for removal
Verify an error message is displayed at the top of the page

title: Recover after an error by retrying the action
description: This test verifies that the app can recover from an error state when the user retries the same action.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Verify the todo list is displayed
Trigger an operation error through your own action
Verify an error message is displayed at the top of the page
Retry the same action
Verify the app returns to the normal todo list state

title: Reject a whitespace-only todo submission
description: This test verifies that submitting whitespace only does not add a new item and shows validation feedback.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill in the todo input with whitespace only
Submit the new todo
Verify a validation error is visible
Verify no new todo is added

title: Retry a failed delete operation
description: This test verifies that after a delete error, the user can retry the action and recover successfully.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Verify the todo list is displayed
Select a todo for removal
Verify an error message is displayed at the top of the page
Retry the delete action
Verify the todo disappears from the list

title: Show an error banner at the top after a failed operation
description: This test verifies that a failed user action surfaces an error message at the top of the page.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Verify the todo list is displayed
Trigger an operation error through your own action
Verify an error message is displayed at the top of the page
